reason_thou:
1. Understanding the Issue:
The loop-vectorizer crashes when destroying a VPReplicateRecipe (for the scalar compare %c) during VPlanTransforms::replicateByVF. The assertion "trying to delete a VPValue with remaining users" indicates the recipe still has users when erased. This happens inside replicateByVF(), which transforms VPReplicateRecipe nodes into per-lane scalar clones and then removes the original recipe. Some users of the original replicated value aren't updated to use the new per-lane values (or a constructed vector from them), leaving dangling uses on the original VPValue.

2. Analyzing opt's Log:
- VPlan shows: REPLICATE ir<%c> = fcmp ogt ir<%l>, ...; WIDEN-SELECT ir<%base> = select ir<%c>, ...
- %c is scalarized (a VPReplicateRecipe). The widen select uses %c directly as its condition operand.
- replicateByVF logs show it's the pass that runs before the crash; the backtrace pinpoints eraseFromParent() called from replicateByVF.

3. Root Cause Analysis:
Within replicateByVF(), for a VPReplicateRecipe RepR, we:
- Create per-lane clones (LaneDefs).
- Replace uses that only demand the first lane with LaneDefs[0].
- Special-case BuildVector/BuildStructVector users to expand their operands to all lanes.
- Then erase RepR at the end of the pass.

However, users that expect a vector operand (e.g., WIDEN-SELECT, or other widen recipes) and currently refer directly to RepR are not updated. In our reproducer, WIDEN-SELECT still uses the original %c after we've created per-lane clones, so when we erase RepR, it still has users, triggering the assertion in VPValue::~VPValue.

The code assumes all remaining users will either be first-lane-only (handled), build vectors (handled), or will themselves be VPReplicateRecipes processed later; but the widen instructions are neither: they are VPInstruction users that expect a vector, and we must provide them a vector value (a buildvector of the lane defs) and redirect their uses before deleting RepR.

4. Proposed Edit Point(s):
File: llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlanUnroll.cpp
Location: In VPlanTransforms::replicateByVF, after creating LaneDefs for a RepR and after updating Build(Vector|StructVector) users, but before pushing RepR to ToRemove (around lines 553–581).

Change: After computing LaneDefs and adjusting Build(Vector|StructVector) users, if RepR still has users, insert a BuildVector VPInstruction from the lane defs and replace remaining uses of RepR with that BuildVector, excluding VPReplicateRecipe users (which will be transformed later using Def2LaneDefs). This ensures widen recipes (like select/icmp/fcmp/etc.) receive a proper vector operand and that RepR ends up with no users by the time we erase it.

Pseudo-code to add before `ToRemove.push_back(RepR);`:

- if (RepR->getNumUsers()) {
    VPInstruction *BV = Builder.createNaryOp(VPInstruction::BuildVector, LaneDefs);
    RepR->replaceUsesWithIf(BV, [](VPUser &U, unsigned) { return !isa<VPReplicateRecipe>(&U); });
  }

This keeps VPReplicateRecipe users intact (they will be processed and remapped to per-lane operands using Def2LaneDefs), and redirects all other remaining users (notably widen instructions like Select) to a vector built from the lanes.

5. Conclusion:
The crash is caused by replicateByVF erasing a VPReplicateRecipe that still has live users (e.g., WIDEN-SELECT conditions). By constructing a BuildVector from the per-lane clones and redirecting non-Replicate users to it before erasing the original recipe, we guarantee Users.empty() holds, preventing the assertion. This also preserves correct semantics for widen recipes expecting vector operands, while allowing replicate users to be transformed later in the same pass.
